sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.95 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Is there an almost simple but not simple group which is recognizable by the isomorphism type of its Gruenberg--Kegel graph? Note that if a group$G$is recognizable by the isomorphism type of its Gruenberg--Kegel graph, then$G$is recognizable by its Gruenberg--Kegel graph, and therefore$G$is almost simple (P.\,J.\,Cameron, N.\,V.\,Maslova, J.~Algebra, { 607} (2022), 186--213).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
